claim: P3 (prior 40%) resolved TRUE: the winning code is the AI/LLM-layer proposal, refereed by its own mechanical verifier; the win exists only because the verifier killed the first proposed family (structural d<=4 ceiling) before any simulation.

P4 (prior 20%) resolved FALSE: the winning family's overhead grows with distance; the published cellular-automaton frontier overhead (about 7.6 qubits/logical at 1e-8, Ruiz et al.) is unmatched at weight-4 checks.

Central measured lesson: codes must match their bias regime -- the winner inverts to 12x/63x worse than repetition at 10x-dirtier gates, and at today's Ocelot bias both architectures drown in the unprotected ~2-5%/cycle bit-flip floor. Model-level.
